assertion_results.text: Sibling check correctly classifies TC-8001 as a different-stream companion (stream [rhtpa-2.2] vs current issue's [rhtpa-2.1]), not a same-stream duplicate (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 18-26: 'The streams are **different** (`2.1.x` vs `2.2.x`). TC-8001 is classified as a **different-stream companion**, NOT a same-stream duplicate.' Explicitly classifies TC-8001 as different-stream companion with correct stream comparison.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 checks the current issue's existing issuelinks before attempting to create a 'Related' link to TC-8001 (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 34-46: 'Before attempting to create a "Related" link to TC-8001, checking the current issue's existing `issuelinks` array (fetched in Step 1):' followed by a table showing existing link ID 1990401, type Related, direction outward (TC-8006 -> TC-8001). The check explicitly precedes any link creation attempt.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 detects the pre-existing 'Related' link to TC-8001 and skips link creation — does NOT call jira.create_link (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 44-50: '**All criteria satisfied.** A matching "Related" link to TC-8001 already exists on this issue.' and 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping link creation.' and 'No call to `jira.create_link` is made. The existing link (ID: 1990401) already establishes the cross-stream relationship.'

assertion_results.text: The output includes a log message indicating the link was skipped because it already exists (e.g., 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists — skipping')
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md line 48: '> Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping link creation.' This blockquote-formatted log message matches the expected pattern. Also repeated in triage-outcome.md line 26: '> Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping link creation.'

assertion_results.text: The sibling landscape table is still presented to the engineer despite the link already existing — the idempotent check only affects link creation, not the sibling summary
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 63-77: Sibling landscape table is presented showing TC-8001 (2.2.x, In Progress, Related pre-existing) and TC-8006 (2.1.x, New, current issue). Line 78: 'The idempotent link check only affects link creation -- it does not suppress the sibling landscape summary. The engineer needs visibility into the full companion issue landscape regardless of whether links were newly created or already existed.'
